evidence.claim_text: The curve for the case with no controller is similar to previous data for the two-fluid MSBR,2 although the magnitude of the peak gain at about 3 radians/sec ( \sim 0.5 \, \text{Hz} ) was greater by a factor of about 6 for the single-fluid case. Adding the servo controller greatly decreased the low-frequency gain, as expected; above the upper cutoff frequency of 5 Hz ( \sim 30 \, \text{radians/sec} ) for the servo it had no effect.

evidence.claim_text: It flows through a 6 in. plenum containing 85 percent salt and 15 percent graphite formed by the 5 in. support legs. The reactor vessel cylinder wall is cooled by a bypass flow of 140\mathrm{gpm} which is diverted from the inlet nozzles and flows up in the gap between the thermal shield and the reactor vessel wall. Our calculations indicate that following a scram the fuel salt outlet temperature falls from 1300\mathrm{F} to 1050\mathrm{F} in less than 10 seconds. For the thickness of pipe in the primary system, this is equivalent to a step change of 250\mathrm{F} . The top reflector graphite is cooled by a stream of 1050\mathrm{F} salt injected through the control rod drive penetration. The inlet fluid enters the reactor at 1050\mathrm{F} . The vessel cooling flow rises 50\mathrm{F} to 1100\mathrm{F} as it is collected at the outlet nozzles. The 1100\mathrm{F} fluid is divided into four streams of 35\mathrm{gpm} each, which is passed between the outlet nozzle and the transition nozzle liner extension.
evidence.title: 1000 MW(e) MOLTEN SALT BREEDER REACTOR

evidence.claim_text: As a result, interest in the thermal molten salt breeder reactor (MSBR) has centered on its special usefulness for breeding on the \mathrm{Th} / ^{233}\mathrm{U} cycle. The \mathrm{Th} / ^{233}\mathrm{U} and the ^{238}\mathrm{U} / \mathrm{Pu} cycles differ in their neutron economics. Interest in the MSFR has focused on exceptional breeding gain (BG) with the ^{238}\mathrm{U} / \mathrm{Pu} cycle [MSFR(U/Pu)]: up to 0.6-0.8. For an MSFR (Th) even with an anticipated reduction of 0.2-0.3, BG= 0.3-0.5 may be possible.
evidence.section: 1.1.3 Technical Solution: Molten Salt Fast Reactor

evidence.claim_text: As a result, the 580^{\circ}\mathrm{F} cycle is estimated to have a total construction cost, including indirect charges, of about half a million dollars less than for the 700^{\circ}\mathrm{F} system.[88] Taking fixed charges at 13.7\% per annum, the saving amounts to about \ 68,500 per year. Based on power worth 4 mills/kWhr, the value of 10 MW(e) at 80\% plant factor is about \ 280,000 per year. The present worth (discounted at 6\% ) over a 30-year plant life of this yearly sum is equivalent to roughly \ 5 million for an MSBR station. Comparing the 580 and 700^{\circ}\mathrm{F} cases in Table 5.4, the lower temperature affords a higher efficiency, which can amount to about 10 MW(e) of additional output capacity. An additional high-pressure feedwater heater is required to obtain the 580^{\circ}\mathrm{F} water, but this cost is more than offset by the expense of the mixing chamber, pressure booster pumps, and reheat steam preheaters needed in the 700^{\circ}\mathrm{F} cycle. The total yearly saving of the lower temperature system is thus about \ 350,000 .

evidence.claim_text: The reactor consists of a 2-in.-thick cylindrical vessel (nominally 22 ft OD × 20 ft high) supported from the bottom. Salt enters through four inlet nozzles at the bottom at 1050^{\circ}\mathrm{F} and exits through four outlet nozzles at the top. The vessel contains 415 tons of graphite which defines salt flow channels in the following regions: core, axial and radial blankets, inlet and outlet salt plena, and axial and radial neutron reflectors. This was done by preserving the salt composition, the region salt fractions, and region dimensions specified in the ORNL reference concept (case CC-120).
evidence.title: MOLTEN-SALT REACTOR PROGRAM
